title: Ukraine Strikes Russia's Primorsk Oil Port and Shadow Fleet Tankers in Major Drone Barrage
summary: Ukrainian forces struck Russia's largest Baltic Sea oil export port at Primorsk, hitting a Karakurt-class missile ship, patrol boat, oil tanker, and two shadow fleet tankers near Novorossiysk, in one of the war's most significant energy infrastructure attacks.
contents:
## Massive Ukrainian Drone Campaign Targets Russian Oil Export Network

### Primorsk Strike
Ukrainian drones attacked the Primorsk oil terminal overnight on May 2-3, igniting fires at the port. NASA FIRMS satellite data confirmed active fire signatures at the terminal. Leningrad Oblast Governor Drozdenko acknowledged Primorsk represented "the key target" of the overnight attack, reporting over 60 drones intercepted.

### Vessels Hit
President Zelensky announced Ukrainian forces destroyed or damaged:
- A Karakurt-class missile ship (Kalibr cruise missile carrier)
- A Russian patrol boat
- An oil tanker at Primorsk
- Two shadow fleet tankers near Novorossiysk on the Black Sea

Zelensky declared: "These tankers had actively transported oil — now they won't."

### Scale and Impact
Primorsk handles approximately 1 million barrels per day of Russian crude — roughly 20% of seaborne oil exports. Previous March-April strikes destroyed at least eight 50,000-cubic-meter storage tanks, eliminating approximately 40% of terminal storage capacity. Russia's Defense Ministry claimed 334 Ukrainian drones intercepted overnight across 16 regions.

### Market Implications
Kremlin spokesman Peskov warned oil prices — already above $120/barrel — could rise further. He paradoxically noted that "even with lower export volumes, our companies would earn more money." St. Petersburg's Pulkovo Airport suspended operations during the attack.

**Sources:** Reuters, ABC News/AP, Bloomberg, Euromaidan Press
